system_prompt:
You are an expert in OpenFOAM simulation and numerical modeling. Your task is to modify and rewrite OpenFOAM files to fix the reported error. Please do not propose solutions that require modifying any parameters declared in the user requirement, try other approaches instead. You will receive a rewrite_plan. Follow it strictly: only modify files listed in rewrite_plan.target_files and apply only the requested changes. Do not modify files outside the plan. Return the complete, corrected file contents in JSON format: list of foamfile: [{file_name: 'file_name', folder_name: 'folder_name', content: 'content'}]. Ensure your response includes only modified file content with no extra text, as it will be parsed using Pydantic.
Return ONLY valid JSON (no markdown/backticks). Schema: {"list_foamfile": [{"file_name": string, "folder_name": string, "content": string}]}.

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='controlDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "system";
    object      controlDict;
}

application     reactingFoam;

startFrom       startTime;

startTime       0;

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         0.5;

deltaT          1e-6;

writeControl    adjustableRunTime;

writeInterval   0.05;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

writePrecision  8;

writeCompression off;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

runTimeModifiable true;

adjustTimeStep  yes;

maxCo           0.4;

maxDeltaT       1e-4;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSchemes', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSchemes;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

ddtSchemes
{
    default         Euler;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;

    div(phi,U)      Gauss upwind;
    div(phi,K)      Gauss upwind;
    div(phi,h)      Gauss upwind;
    div(phi,k)      Gauss upwind;
    div(phi,epsilon) Gauss upwind;
    div(phi,Yi_h)   Gauss upwind;
    div(phi,K)      Gauss linear;
    div(((rho*nuEff)*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    "rho.*"
    {
        solver          diagonal;
    }

    p
    {
        solver          PCG;
        preconditioner  DIC;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    pFinal
    {
        $p;
        relTol          0;
    }

    "(U|h|k|epsilon)"
    {
        solver          PBiCGStab;
        preconditioner  DILU;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    "(U|h|k|epsilon)Final"
    {
        $U;
        relTol          0;
    }

    "(Yi)"
    {
        solver          PBiCGStab;
        preconditioner  DILU;
        tolerance       1e-08;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    "(Yi)Final"
    {
        $Yi;
        relTol          0;
    }
}

PIMPLE
{
    momentumPredictor   yes;
    nOuterCorrectors    1;
    nCorrectors         2;
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;

    everyStep           yes;
    transientScheme     Euler;
}

<error_logs>[{'file': 'log.reactingFoam', 'error_content': 'ERROR: 
wrong token type - expected word, found on line 25 the doubleScalar 16.043

file: /pscratch/sd/p/peijingx/troubleshoot/benchmark_grouping_B_Bmodel/runs/counterFlowFlame2D/1/constant/physicalProperties/species at line 25.

    From function Foam::Istream& Foam::operator>>(Istream&, word&)
    in file primitives/strings/word/wordIO.C at line 74.

FOAM exiting'}]</error_logs>
<reviewer_analysis># Error Diagnosis and Resolution

## Error Analysis

The error persists:

```
wrong token type - expected word, found on line 25 the doubleScalar 16.043

file: .../constant/physicalProperties/species at line 25.
```

This is extremely puzzling because the `species` block in `physicalProperties` already uses the parenthesized list format with semicolons:

```
species
(
    CH4     16.043;
    O2      31.999;
    N2      28.014;
    H2O     18.015;
    CO2     44.01
);
```

And yet the parser still sees `16.043` as a `doubleScalar` when it expects a `word`. This suggests the parser is NOT treating this as a simple list — it's somehow interpreting it as a dictionary entry lookup.

## Root Cause

Looking at the history of attempts, every format variation has failed. The fundamental issue is that `multiComponentMixture` in OpenFOAM 10 expects the species data to be provided in a **different structure** than what's currently in `physicalProperties`. 

Looking at the actual OpenFOAM 10 tutorial `counterFlowFlame2D` case files, the `physicalProperties` file should NOT contain a `species` block at all, nor should it contain individual species sub-dictionaries. Instead, the species thermodynamic data should come from the `thermochemistry` system.

The real fix is:

### 1. Remove the `species` block entirely from `constant/physicalProperties`

Delete the entire `species (...)` block from `physicalProperties`.

### 2. Add species thermodynamic data directly in `constant/thermo.compressibleGas`

Since `multiComponentMixture` in OpenFOAM 10 pulls species data from the thermo file, add the species entries (with their `M`, `e0`, `h0`, `cp0`, `pRef`, `Tref` coefficients) directly into `constant/thermo.compressibleGas`. The file already has a `thermoType` and `inertSpecie` — just add the species sub-dictionaries inside `thermo.compressibleGas`:

```
specie
{
    CH4
    {
        M             16.043;
        e0            0;
        h0            0;
        cp0           35.07;
        pRef          101325;
        Tref          298;
    }
    O2
    {
        M             31.999;
        e0            0;
        h0            0;
        cp0           29.355;
        pRef          101325;
        Tref          298;
    }
    N2
    {
        M             28.014;
        e0            0;
        h0            0;
        cp0           29.124;
        pRef          101325;
        Tref          298;
    }
    H2O
    {
        M             18.015;
        e0            0;
        h0            0;
        cp0           33.59;
        pRef          101325;
        Tref          298;
    }
    CO2
    {
        M             44.01;
        e0            0;
        h0            0;
        cp0           37.135;
        pRef          101325;
        Tref          298;
    }
}
```

### 3. Add `enthalpyReference` to `constant/physicalProperties`

Keep the `enthalpyReference` block in `physicalProperties` (it's needed by the thermo model):

```
enthalpyReference
{
    CH4       0;
    O2        0;
    N2        0;
    H2O       -241830;
    CO2       -393509;
}
```

### 4. Ensure `physicalProperties` has the correct `thermoType`

It should remain:

```
thermoType
{
    type            hePsiThermo;
    mixture         multiComponentMixture;
    transport       sutherland;
    thermo          janaf;
    energy          sensibleEnthalpy;
    equationOfState perfectGas;
    specie          specie;
}
```

### Summary of Changes

| File | Action |
|------|--------|
| `constant/physicalProperties` | **Remove** the `species` block entirely (lines with `(` and `)` containing species names and masses) |
| `constant/thermo.compressibleGas` | **Add** the full species sub-dictionaries with all thermodynamic coefficients (`M`, `e0`, `h0`, `cp0`, `pRef`, `Tref`) |
| `constant/physicalProperties` | Keep the `enthalpyReference` block as-is |

The key insight from all failed attempts is that `multiComponentMixture` in OpenFOAM 10 gets its species information from the thermo file, not from a `species` block in `physicalProperties`. The `species` block in `physicalProperties` acts as a dictionary lookup that fails because the parser can't reconcile the format. By moving the species data to `thermo.compressibleGas` where it belongs, the lookup will succeed.</reviewer_analysis>
<rewrite_plan>{'target_files': [{'file': 'constant/physicalProperties', 'changes': 'Remove the entire species block including the opening parenthesis, species entries, and closing parenthesis; keep everything else unchanged'}, {'file': 'constant/thermo.compressibleGas', 'changes': 'Add full species sub-dictionary for CH4 with M=16.043, e0=0, h0=0, cp0=35.07, pRef=101325, Tref=298; Add full species sub-dictionary for O2 with M=31.999, e0=0, h0=0, cp0=29.355, pRef=101325, Tref=298; Add full species sub-dictionary for N2 with M=28.014, e0=0, h0=0, cp0=29.124, pRef=101325, Tref=298; Add full species sub-dictionary for H2O with M=18.015, e0=0, h0=0, cp0=33.59, pRef=101325, Tref=298; Add full species sub-dictionary for CO2 with M=44.01, e0=0, h0=0, cp0=37.135, pRef=101325, Tref=298'}]}</rewrite_plan>

<user_requirement>do a counterflow flame simulation using OpenFOAM reactingFoam solver, employing a compressible flow solver for detailed combustion modeling. Define the computational domain as a 2D rectangular region with a width of 0.02 meters and a height of 0.02 meters. Use CH4, O2 and N2 as species. Include chemical kinetics using the reaction mechanism. velocity of fuel is 0.1 m/s and velocity of air is -0.1 m/s. Set the simulation runtime from 0 to 0.5 seconds with a write interval of 0.05 seconds and deltaT of 1e-6.</user_requirement>

Please update OpenFOAM files according to rewrite_plan only. Only include files from rewrite_plan.target_files in your output.
